person.name: Xujie Fang
person.tagline: undergraduate exploring chemistry, modeling, and research workflows

highlights.title: Academic Strength
highlights.desc: GPA 3.60, ranked 1st in class and 2nd in major, with scholarship and merit-student recognition.

highlights.title: Modeling Training
highlights.desc: CUMCM Jiangsu Provincial First Prize, built through repeated practice in problem framing, assumptions, analysis, and writing.

highlights.title: International Exposure
highlights.desc: Consecutive MCM/ICM Honorable Mentions in 2025 and 2026, with experience in English academic modeling papers.

highlights.title: Campus Leadership
highlights.desc: Class academic representative, teaching information liaison, debate runner-up, and campus sports event organizer.

competitions.title: MathorCup Math Modeling Challenge, Regional 2nd Prize
Baima Campus Freshman Volleyball Cup, Runner-up

skills.desc: Organic chemistry mechanisms and chemical engineering fundamentals.
Mathematical modeling, scientific writing, and data visualization.
Frontend fundamentals and AI-assisted development workflows.
Office, drawing, simulation, and reference management tools.

projects.title: Mathematical Modeling Practice
projects.desc: Worked on open-ended modeling problems across CUMCM, MCM/ICM, and MathorCup. The practice emphasized problem decomposition, assumption design, data processing, result interpretation, and paper writing.
projects.abilities: Problem framing, model assumptions, data analysis, academic writing

projects.title: AI-Assisted Learning & Knowledge Management
projects.desc: Built a personal workflow for literature reading and knowledge organization with AI tools, Zotero, Obsidian, Markdown, and LaTeX, aiming to make coursework and research preparation more traceable.
projects.abilities: Reference management, knowledge organization, research communication, learning reflection
projects.todo: Obsidian vault screenshots, reading note samples, workflow documentation

projects.title: Personal Academic Resume Website
projects.desc: Built a bilingual academic resume website with Astro, including portfolio sections, an evidence gallery, project pages, responsive layouts, print-friendly resumes, and automated quality checks.
projects.abilities: Component architecture, responsive design, static deployment, information architecture, privacy-aware versioning, CI/CD

projects.title: ChemAI Lab
projects.desc: A machine learning & quantum chemistry driven chemical research platform. Integrates RDKit, PyTorch, Lightning across 15 subpackages for data, features, modeling, XAI, and QM interfaces.
projects.abilities: Python, PyTorch, RDKit, ML Engineering, Project Architecture

experience.title: Class Academic Representative
experience.desc: Responsible for assignment collection and distribution, academic affairs coordination, and supporting course instructors.

experience.title: Teaching Information Liaison
experience.desc: Responsible for communicating teaching information, collecting course feedback, and facilitating student-teacher communication.

volunteer.summary: 2024: Primary organizer of the 1st Baima Campus Freshman Volleyball Cup
2024: Participated in the 'Card House' volunteer service activity
2025: Participated in the Red Cross voluntary blood donation activity

researchInterests.tagline: Organic chemistry as the foundation, computational tools as the extension
researchInterests.summary: My current academic interest starts from organic reaction mechanisms and synthetic strategy, then extends toward computational chemistry and AI-assisted research workflows. I am still at an early undergraduate stage, so I treat this direction as a long-term training path: build a solid chemistry foundation first, then learn modeling, programming, and literature workflows that can support future research.

areas.name: Organic Synthesis Methodology
areas.note: Ongoing study of Clayden, March, and related texts, with focus on synthetic strategy and reaction mechanisms

areas.name: Computational Chemistry / AI-Assisted Synthesis
areas.note: Long-term interest in reaction prediction, retrosynthetic planning, and molecular property prediction

areas.name: LLMs + Agent Workflows
areas.note: Exploring how LLMs can support literature retrieval, reading notes, and research knowledge management

areas.name: Deep Learning Fundamentals
areas.note: Studying neural network basics, with interest in graph neural networks for molecular representation learning
